---
title: "Arriola_randforest_reg"
author: "Arriola, Phoebe M."
date: "2026-07-13"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup-and-data}
# 1. Setting up packages 
library(ranger)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
getwd()
setwd("/Users/phoebearriola/Desktop/uni/LBBBI27")
list.files()

# 2. Read in the acoustic data and remove unnecessary columns (i.e., column 1). Remember that your response variable is the species column.
df <- read.csv("call_data_12October2025.csv")
df_clean <- df[, -1]
df_clean$Species <- as.factor(df_clean$Species)
str(df_clean)

# 3. Design your Cartesian grid and perform a random forest that iterates on each hyperparameter combination.
grid <- expand.grid(
  num.trees = c(100, 300, 500),
  mtry = c(2, 4, 6),
  min.node.size = c(1, 5, 10)
)
grid$OOB_Error <- NA

print(head(grid))

set.seed(123)
for(i in 1:nrow(grid)) {
rf_fit <- ranger(
    formula         = Species ~ ., 
    data            = df_clean, 
    num.trees       = grid$num.trees[i],
    mtry            = grid$mtry[i],
    min.node.size   = grid$min.node.size[i],
    probability     = FALSE # Standard classification to get clear OOB error
  )
grid$OOB_Error[i] <- rf_fit$prediction.error
}
print(grid)

# 4. Pick the best hyperparameter combination that minimizes the out-of-bag error.
best_row <- grid[which.min(grid$OOB_Error), ]
print("Best Hyperparameter Combination Found:")
print(best_row) 

# 5. Using the optimal hyperparameter, provide a plot of variable importance and the predicted class probabilities.

## Variable Importance Plot
optimal_rf <- ranger(
  formula         = Species ~ ., 
  data            = df_clean, 
  num.trees       = best_row$num.trees,
  mtry            = best_row$mtry,
  min.node.size   = best_row$min.node.size,
  importance      = "permutation"
)

imp_scores <- importance(optimal_rf)
imp_df <- data.frame(
  Variable = names(imp_scores),
  Importance = as.numeric(imp_scores)
) %>% arrange(desc(Importance))

ggplot(imp_df, aes(x = reorder(Variable, Importance), y = Importance)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Variable Importance Plot (Optimal Model)",
       x = "Predictors",
       y = "Permutation Importance Score") +
  theme_minimal()

## Predicted Class Probabilities Plot
prob_rf <- ranger(
  formula         = Species ~ ., 
  data            = df_clean, 
  num.trees       = best_row$num.trees,
  mtry            = best_row$mtry,
  min.node.size   = best_row$min.node.size,
  probability     = TRUE
)

predicted_probs <- predict(prob_rf, data = df_clean)$predictions
head(predicted_probs)

prob_df <- data.frame(predicted_probs)
prob_df$Actual_Species <- df_clean$Species

prob_long <- prob_df %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols = -Actual_Species, 
    names_to = "Predicted_Species", 
    values_to = "Probability"
  )

ggplot(prob_long, aes(x = Predicted_Species, y = Probability, fill = Predicted_Species)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  facet_wrap(~ Actual_Species) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Predicted Class Probabilities by Actual Species",
       x = "Predicted Species Class",
       y = "Predicted Probability Allocation") +
  theme_light() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
